subUnits.title: 11.1 Passé Composé with AVOIR (Regular & Irregular)
notes: - Example: J'ai mangé (I ate), Tu as fini (You finished).

- Mastering AVOIR is crucial for the BECE objective section. Students often confuse the infinitive (manger) with the past participle (mangé). Remember: 'J'ai mangé' is correct; 'J'ai manger' is a fatal error in the exam!
diagramDescriptions: Table of regular and irregular past participles with AVOIR
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French revision passe compose avoir irregular past participles site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What is the past participle of the regular -ER verb 'manger'?
quiz.explanation: Regular -ER verbs drop the -er and add -é. Manger becomes mangé.

quiz.q: What is the past participle of 'finir' (to finish)?
quiz.explanation: Regular -IR verbs drop the -ir and add -i. Finir becomes fini.

quiz.q: What is the past participle of 'vendre' (to sell)?
quiz.explanation: Regular -RE verbs drop the -re and add -u. Vendre becomes vendu.

quiz.q: What is the irregular past participle of 'faire' (to do/make)?
quiz.explanation: Faire is irregular; its past participle is fait.

quiz.q: Complete: 'J'_______ mangé du riz.'
quiz.explanation: The subject is 'Je' (J'), so the correct auxiliary is 'ai'.

quiz.q: What is the irregular past participle of 'voir' (to see)?
quiz.explanation: The past participle of voir is vu.

quiz.q: What is the past participle of 'prendre' (to take)?
quiz.explanation: Prendre is irregular; its past participle is pris.

quiz.q: What is the past participle of 'avoir' (to have)?
quiz.explanation: Avoir is highly irregular; its past participle is eu.

quiz.q: What is the past participle of 'lire' (to read)?
quiz.explanation: The past participle of lire is lu.

quiz.q: What is the past participle of 'mettre' (to put)?
quiz.explanation: The past participle of mettre is mis.

subUnits.title: 11.2 Passé Composé with ÊTRE and Agreement
notes: - Most are verbs of movement or change of state (e.g., aller, venir, partir, arriver, naître, mourir).
- Example: Je suis allé (I went), Il est venu (He came).
- With ÊTRE, the past participle MUST agree in gender and number with the subject!

- In the BECE theory section, failing to add the extra '-e' for a female subject (e.g., 'Marie est allé') will cost you marks. Always check the gender of the subject before writing your past participle!
diagramDescriptions: DR MRS VANDERTRAMP chart with agreement endings
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French revision passe compose etre agreement DR MRS VANDERTRAMP site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Which auxiliary verb is used with 'aller' (to go) in the passé composé?
quiz.explanation: Aller is a movement verb and uses Être as its auxiliary.

quiz.q: What mnemonic is used to remember the 16 ÊTRE verbs?
quiz.explanation: DR & MRS VANDERTRAMP is the classic mnemonic for the 16 verbs that use Être.

quiz.q: How do you write 'She went' (Elle est allé) correctly with agreement?
quiz.explanation: Elle is feminine singular, so we add -e to the past participle: allée.

quiz.q: Complete: 'Je _______ allé au marché.' (Addressing a male)
quiz.explanation: The subject is 'Je', so the correct auxiliary is 'suis'.

quiz.q: How do you write 'They (males) left' correctly?
quiz.explanation: Ils is masculine plural, so we add -s: partis.

quiz.q: How do you write 'They (females) came' correctly?
quiz.explanation: Elles is feminine plural, so we add -es: venues.

quiz.q: How do you write 'She stayed' correctly?
quiz.explanation: Rester uses Être. Elle is feminine singular, so we add -e: restée.

quiz.q: How do you write 'We (mixed group) went up' correctly?
quiz.explanation: Nous (mixed group) is masculine plural, so we add -s: montés.

quiz.q: How do you write 'The girls went down' (Elles sont descendre) correctly?
quiz.explanation: Elles is feminine plural, so we add -es: descendues.

quiz.q: How do you write 'They (males) arrived' correctly?
quiz.explanation: Ils is masculine plural, so we add -s: arrivés.

subUnits.title: 11.3 Common Mistakes and BECE Objective Practice
notes: 1. Mistake 1: Using the infinitive instead of the past participle.
- WRONG: J'ai manger. (I have to eat.)
- WRONG: Elle est allé au marché.
- CORRECT: Elle est allée au marché.
- WRONG: Nous avons allé à l'école.
- CORRECT: Nous sommes allés à l'école.
4. Mistake 4: Placing 'pas' after the past participle.
- BECE examiners specifically design objective questions to test these exact common mistakes. If you see 'J'ai manger' as an option, eliminate it immediately!
diagramDescriptions: Side-by-side comparison of common mistakes vs correct forms
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French common mistakes passe compose BECE objective practice site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Which of the following is INCORRECT?
quiz.explanation: J'ai manger is incorrect. It must be the past participle: J'ai mangé.

quiz.q: Which of the following is INCORRECT?
quiz.explanation: Elle est allé is incorrect. It requires the feminine agreement: Elle est allée.

quiz.q: Which of the following is INCORRECT?
quiz.explanation: Nous avons allés is incorrect. Aller uses Être, not Avoir.

quiz.q: Which of the following sentences is 100% CORRECT?
quiz.explanation: Ils sont partis is correct. The others have agreement or auxiliary errors.

quiz.q: Which auxiliary does the verb 'partir' (to leave) take?
quiz.explanation: Partir is a movement verb and uses Être.

quiz.q: Which auxiliary does the verb 'regarder' (to watch) take?
quiz.explanation: Regarder is a regular action verb and uses Avoir (J'ai regardé).

quiz.q: Which auxiliary does the verb 'tomber' (to fall) take?
quiz.explanation: Tomber is a verb of movement/change of state and uses Être.

quiz.q: Which auxiliary does the verb 'choisir' (to choose) take?
quiz.explanation: Choisir is a regular -IR verb and uses Avoir (J'ai choisi).

quiz.q: Which auxiliary does the verb 'naître' (to be born) take?
quiz.explanation: Naître is a change of state verb and uses Être (Il est né).

quiz.q: Which auxiliary does the verb 'attendre' (to wait) take?
quiz.explanation: Attendre is a regular -RE verb and uses Avoir (J'ai attendu).
